SPACE FACTS: DAY 84/365🔭🧪🛰️ The closest planet to Earth on average? Mercury! Venus is the planet that *gets* the closest to Earth, but it also spends time on the far side of the Sun. Because it stays close to the Sun, Mercury is actually the closest planet to every other planet on average.

SPACE FACTS: DAY 85/365🔭🧪🛰️ Because heavier elements are created as the universe ages, stars that are born later have more metals in them. Stars like our Sun, which are relatively young and metal rich, belong to a class of stars called Population I.

They suppose understanding lengths of sides on a right triangle requires use of the theorem, but that's just not true, and it's equivocating on the ancient world's understanding of discrete numerals. The tablet does support the orientalist nature of pre euclidean science though.
W5.action_text: Not sure I understand what you mean. Any method of calculating the sides of a right triangle is mathematically equivalent to the theorem.

W5.target: Put it another way, this shows they knew and used some sets of triangle side proportions (triples) which is neat but you could get those by just looking.
W5.action_text: Oh I see. You're saying how do we know they were able to determine these values in advance, as opposed to just measuring them

W5.target:
Yeah.  It's kind of unfair to look at math not from the frame of mind of whoever wrote the tablet.

They were probably using base 60, which will lend itself to different patterns in counting such as divisions of 360 or 30, which work for calendars. That doesn't mean they worked out "january."
W5.action_text: "January" is a quirk of history not a mathematical truth so still not sure what you're saying. If they had a method for calculating the third side of a right triangle given the other two then that's = Pythagoras regardless of how they expressed it. But maybe they didn't. That's the fair point.

W1.action_text: I am begging the community to stop filling aspiring writers' heads with so many BS 'rules'. Writing is not math, it's not a colour by numbers exercise. It's about expression. Play with words, bend them, break them, make them fit your expression!

W1.action_text: I remember a time when I actually really enjoyed discussing things online. But I think that time basically ended with the triumph of social media. To have a decent discussion, you need a focused forum, with some decorum. Not this free for all bullshit.

W1.target:
I think a lot of people are missing the fact that the younger generation is policing the hell out of what media people consume.

The "allowed" here is just as much rules as it is am I allowed to do Z, Y, or Z within this framework of purity.
W1.action_text: I really hate to talk about young people, and generalize them, but... god the amount of young people I see trying to put all of existence into categories, and reduce everything to simple rules, is so painful. It's not just writing, it's every form of expression, every part of the human experience.

SPACE FACTS: DAY 103/365🧪🛰️🔭 There's lots of solar system beyond the planets, like all the small bodies beyond Neptune called trans-Neptunian objects. These are generally either in the Kuiper Belt or the scattered disk. Groups in the Kuiper Belt include plutinos, cubewanos, & twotinos! Image: NASA

SPACE FACTS: DAY 104/365🧪🛰️🔭 There are two basic causes of supernovae—thermal runway or core collapse. Confusingly, these two causes do not correspond with the two *types* of supernovae, Type I and Type II, which were classified based on the presence of hydrogen in their spectra 🙃

SPACE FACTS: DAY 105/365 🔭🧪🛰️ The sun doesn't just lose mass from the violent explosions of material called coronal mass ejections. There is a steady flow of plasma from the surface of the sun called the solar wind, and it carries away about 44 trillion tons of material every year.

Honestly I think it is important for British people just to spend a bit of time understanding how much we fucked it up when we waded in going "oh we can fix this, it's easy!"

W4.action_text: Isn’t that almost like making the current generation culpable for the sins of their fathers? The Balfour agreement pre-dates any current decision makers so the real learning is not to treat regional issues with casual ignorance….but what do we do now

W4.target: I mean. If you really want to wade in again and go "we can fix this!" then I can't stop you. My suggestion is that a good period of learning, thinking and grieving about how our culture buggered it up last time *before* trying to *do anything* might be recommended.
W4.action_text: The alternative is standing by and watching. And deciding not to intervene - at great cost.

W4.target: What I'm talking about is not skipping straight over feelings to action. There are a lot of feelings we need to have before we can begin to turn toward actions.

the agèd man sips his tea
As I watch him read his book
he scarcely sees the way I look
across the park and through my lens
at this calming scene of simple Zen
We share the moment nonetheless 
And I enjoy it, I confess

W4.target: This year’s Nobel Prize has been awarded to Katalin Karikó and Drew Weissman, who are the inventors of the Covid-19 mRNA vaccines, a discovery that has saved millions of lives. Congrats!

An important thing to remember about Karikó and Weissman’s Nobel is that for years the scientific establishment didn’t believe that using mRNA for vaccines would work. It almost drove them out of science. Decades later it’s one of the biggest life saving discoveries of our time.

Katalin Karikó was the daughter of a butcher. She wanted to be a scientist, although she had never met one. She immigrated to the US in her 20s, but for decades never found a permanent position, receiving little support from the scientific establishment. Now she’s a Nobel winner!

W2.target: SPACE FACTS: DAY 105/365 🔭🧪🛰️ The sun doesn't just lose mass from the violent explosions of material called coronal mass ejections. There is a steady flow of plasma from the surface of the sun called the solar wind, and it carries away about 44 trillion tons of material every year.

W3.target: SPACE FACTS: DAY 104/365🧪🛰️🔭 There are two basic causes of supernovae—thermal runway or core collapse. Confusingly, these two causes do not correspond with the two *types* of supernovae, Type I and Type II, which were classified based on the presence of hydrogen in their spectra 🙃

IMO this has to start long before the published paper.

W5.target: My editor take on tonight's topic: I want all your characters well-rounded. Does the waitress who drops off the drinks and is never seen again need to have a whole backstory? Probably not. But if you're going to do it, do it right. #kidlitchat
W5.action_text: Sometimes an observation and an insight from the MC’s point of view can inject a little dimension to a side character. But I often prefer getting in the waitress’s head for a moment when the protagonists leave the room. What did SHE think of them? That’s a way to enrich your story!

Insomnia Anatomy Academy 3: 🧪 Your teeth are joints. They're a fibrous joints called dento-alveolar syndesmoses. Science speak for: pegs of tooth fit into jaw socket. LOOSE teeth are bad right? Why's it a joint? Because a tiny bit of movement tells you how hard you're cronching! Proprioception!

Insomnia Anatomy Academy 4: 🧪 This one makes me smile. The resting muscle contraction (muscle tonus) of your LIPS keeps your TEETH in line. Really. One of the causes of buckteeth is a lip that doesn't fully cover the teeth, allowing the front teeth to get all rebellious.

Insomnia Anatomy Academy 9: 🧪 This is a thing I've honestly known for ages, but it just boggles me every time i think about it. The cell bodies of your neurons are your grey matter, right. The white matter are the fiber tracts. So you can literally SEE the ropes of your neurons!
